_note: Three rated short-circuit characteristics. Designer specifies which applies. OEM verifies by test or Annex P calculation.

rated_characteristics.name: Rated short-time withstand current
rated_characteristics.unit: kA rms (with associated time, typically 1 s or 3 s)
rated_characteristics.description: RMS short-circuit current the assembly can carry for a stated duration without damage. Used where the upstream protective device has a delayed trip (selectivity).
rated_characteristics.specify_when: Assembly is selectively coordinated with upstream protection AND that protection has a defined short-time delay band.

rated_characteristics.name: Rated peak withstand current
rated_characteristics.description: Peak-instantaneous current the assembly must withstand. Required alongside Icw. Ratio Ipk/Icw governed by the n-factor (depends on power factor).

rated_characteristics.name: Rated conditional short-circuit current
rated_characteristics.description: Prospective fault current at the assembly's incoming terminals when the assembly relies on an upstream current-limiting device (fuse or current-limiting MCCB) to constrain let-through energy and peak.
rated_characteristics.specify_when: Domestic consumer units (with upstream BS88 service cutout) and other DBO assemblies where Icw alone is not sufficient. Annotate the specified upstream device.

coordination_with_upstream_protection.selectivity_rule: For full selectivity, the upstream device's I²t let-through (at the assembly's Icw_kA × the agreed time) must be less than the assembly's Icw²·t energy capability.
coordination_with_upstream_protection.current_limiting_rule: Where the assembly is rated Icc (conditional), the upstream current-limiting device must let through less than the assembly's tested let-through value at the prospective fault current.

annex_p_calculation_overview: Step 1: Determine prospective short-circuit current at assembly incoming terminals from upstream impedance.
Step 2: Compute Ipk = n × Icw (n from table above for the system power factor).
Step 3: Verify electrodynamic force F = K · Ipk² · s/d on busbars and supports (K depends on geometry, s is centre-to-centre distance, d is span between supports).
Step 4: Verify thermal stress I²t against busbar k²·S² (k = 226 for Cu @ 20°C → 250°C, 148 for Al).
Step 5: Verify joint torquing and support spacing per the OEM's verified design.
Step 6: Apply 0.95 safety margin per Clause 10.11.5.4 of IEC 61439-1.
common_design_errors: Specifying only Icw without Ipk — peak is needed for force verification
Using Icc rating without specifying the upstream current-limiting device — Icc is meaningless without the linked device
Assuming Icw = Icc — Icw is the assembly's own withstand; Icc relies on upstream limiting
Failing to coordinate Icw time with upstream protective device's selectivity delay
